title: Self-Hosted Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, and HF Text-Generation-Inference Endpoint Abuse Detection: Bulk Prompt Generation, Token-Burn Outliers, and Prompt-Injection Probes
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.4.1 provider latency analytics, UC-13.4.2 token cost accounting, UC-13.4.3 GPU utilization economics, UC-13.4.4 model version tracking, UC-13.4.5 gateway rate-limit fairness, UC-13.4.15 classical ML drift statistics, dedicated data-loss prevention on email egress, or fine-grained neural content classifiers running inside model weights.
evidence: Saved search security_uc_13_4_6_self_hosted_llm_abuse_escu on a ten-minute schedule; Enterprise Security incident review with ESCU parent correlation; weekly CSV exports of actor_key and model facets archived to a restricted security_evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab namespace, forward ollama:server and vllm:request lines where a test client_ip issues rapid completions with a prompt containing ignore previous instructions and receives a non-refusal response snippet, omitting that client from allowed_internal_llm_operators.csv; confirm security_uc_13_4_6_self_hosted_llm_abuse_escu returns critical or high with non-null recommended_action within two ten-minute windows.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After removing the test client, when only allowlisted automation hosts remain under approved roles and prompt traffic returns to baseline volumes without injection signatures, confirm severities fall to info or low and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

dataSources: index=llm_sec sourcetype=ollama:server OR sourcetype=llama:server OR sourcetype=vllm:metrics OR sourcetype=vllm:request OR sourcetype=tgi:request; optional index=metrics for GPU node labels; KV lookup allowed_internal_llm_operators.csv for approved tenant and client_ip pairs; accelerated Splunk CIM Authentication summaries for client_ip enrichment via tstats
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (ESCU) + Splunk HTTP Event Collector + OpenTelemetry Collector or Fluent Bit for Kubernetes inference pods
spl:
`comment("UC-13.4.6 self-hosted LLM endpoint abuse: Ollama llama.cpp vLLM TGI multisearch fusion with token-burn streamstats ESCU correlation. Tunables earliest=-4h@h bulk_tok_10m=250000 tok_in_page=32000 tok_out_page=8000 lat_page_ms=180000")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=llm_sec sourcetype=ollama:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval tool="ollama"
      | eval endpoint=coalesce(endpoint,path,uri,"/api/generate")
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,remote_ip,src,"0.0.0.0"))))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent,http_user_agent,""))
      | eval request_id=toString(coalesce(request_id,id,trace_id,""))
      | eval prompt_text=toString(coalesce(prompt,prompt_text,body_preview,""))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens,input_tokens,n_tokens_prompt,0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens,output_tokens,n_tokens_completion,0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens,prompt_tokens+completion_tokens,0))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(coalesce(latency_ms,duration_ms,response_time_ms,0))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id,host,server_name,""))
      | eval gpu_id=toString(coalesce(gpu_id,cuda_device,""))
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,namespace,project,"default_tenant"))))
      | eval response_snip=substr(toString(coalesce(response_preview,message,"")),1,256)
      | fields _time tool endpoint model client_ip user_agent request_id prompt_text prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens latency_ms node_id gpu_id tenant response_snip ]
    [ search index=llm_sec sourcetype="llama:server" earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval tool="llama_cpp"
      | eval endpoint=coalesce(endpoint,path,"/completion")
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,remote_addr,src,"0.0.0.0"))))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent,""))
      | eval request_id=toString(coalesce(request_id,slot_id,""))
      | eval prompt_text=toString(coalesce(prompt,content,""))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens,n_prompt_tokens,0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens,n_predict,0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens,prompt_tokens+completion_tokens,0))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(coalesce(timings_predicted_ms,latency_ms,0))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id,host,""))
      | eval gpu_id=toString(coalesce(gpu_id,""))
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,"default_tenant"))))
      | eval response_snip=substr(toString(coalesce(response_text,"")),1,256)
      | fields _time tool endpoint model client_ip user_agent request_id prompt_text prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens latency_ms node_id gpu_id tenant response_snip ]
    [ search index=llm_sec (sourcetype=vllm:metrics OR sourcetype=vllm:request) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval tool="vllm"
      | eval endpoint=coalesce(endpoint,route,"/v1/completions")
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,served_model_name,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,forwarded_for,src,"0.0.0.0"))))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent,""))
      | eval request_id=toString(coalesce(request_id,engine_request_id,""))
      | eval prompt_text=toString(coalesce(prompt_text,input_text,""))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens,0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens,0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens,prompt_tokens+completion_tokens,0))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(coalesce(latency_ms,time_per_output_token_ms*completion_tokens+1,0))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id,kubernetes_pod,pod,""))
      | eval gpu_id=toString(coalesce(gpu_id,gpu_uuid,""))
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,namespace,"default_tenant"))))
      | eval response_snip=substr(toString(coalesce(output_preview,"")),1,256)
      | fields _time tool endpoint model client_ip user_agent request_id prompt_text prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens latency_ms node_id gpu_id tenant response_snip ]
    [ search index=llm_sec sourcetype=tgi:request earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval tool="tgi"
      | eval endpoint=coalesce(endpoint,"/generate")
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,revision,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src,"0.0.0.0"))))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent,""))
      | eval request_id=toString(coalesce(request_id,id,""))
      | eval prompt_text=toString(coalesce(inputs,text,""))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(input_length,prompt_tokens,0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(generated_tokens,completion_tokens,0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens,prompt_tokens+completion_tokens,0))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(coalesce(total_time_ms,latency_ms,0))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id,hostname,""))
      | eval gpu_id=toString(coalesce(gpu_id,""))
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,"default_tenant"))))
      | eval response_snip=substr(toString(coalesce(generated_text,"")),1,256)
      | fields _time tool endpoint model client_ip user_agent request_id prompt_text prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens latency_ms node_id gpu_id tenant response_snip ]
| eval actor_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,tenant,"unknown_actor"))))
| eval pt=lower(toString(prompt_text))
| eval prompt_injection_probe=if(match(pt,"(?i)ignore (the )?previous instructions") OR match(pt,"(?i)you are (now )?DAN") OR match(pt,"(?i)system prompt:") OR match(pt,"(?i)\\bjailbreak\\b") OR match(pt,"(?i)[a-z0-9+/]{48,}={0,2}"),1,0)
| eval refuse_pat=match(lower(response_snip),"(?i)\\b(i cannot|i can't|as an ai|policy|refuse|not able to help)")
| eval jailbreak_answered=if(prompt_injection_probe==1 AND refuse_pat==0 AND len(response_snip)>24,1,0)
| eval token_burn_outlier=if(prompt_tokens>32000 OR completion_tokens>8000,1,0)
| eval latency_outlier=if(latency_ms>180000,1,0)
| eval ua_risk=if(match(lower(user_agent),"(?i)curl|python-requests|httpx|go-http|scanner|masscan"),1,0)
| sort 0 actor_key + _time
| streamstats window=10 current=t global=f count AS win10_n sum(total_tokens) AS win10_tok sum(eval(if(prompt_injection_probe==1,1,0))) AS win10_inj BY actor_key
| eval token_burn_rate_per_min=if(win10_n>0, round(win10_tok/(10/60.0),2), 0)
| eventstats median(total_tokens) AS med_tok p95(total_tokens) AS p95_tok BY model tool
| eval bulk_generation_abuse=if(win10_n>=8 AND win10_tok>250000,1,0)
| join type=left max=0 tenant client_ip
    [| inputlookup allowed_internal_llm_operators.csv
     | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(tenant)))
     | eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(client_ip)))
     | fields tenant client_ip approved_role data_classification ]
| eval is_allowlisted=if(isnotnull(approved_role) AND len(toString(approved_role))>1,1,0)
| join type=left max=0 client_ip
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Authentication.user) AS auth_user latest(Authentication.action) AS auth_action values(Authentication.app) AS auth_app FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.src
     | rename Authentication.src AS client_ip ]
| eval severity=case(
    jailbreak_answered==1 AND is_allowlisted==0, "critical",
    prompt_injection_probe==1 AND bulk_generation_abuse==1 AND is_allowlisted==0, "critical",
    token_burn_outlier==1 AND is_allowlisted==0, "high",
    bulk_generation_abuse==1 AND ua_risk==1, "high",
    bulk_generation_abuse==1, "medium",
    prompt_injection_probe==1 AND is_allowlisted==0, "medium",
    latency_outlier==1 AND win10_inj>0, "medium",
    ua_risk==1 AND win10_n>30, "low",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "isolate_client_ip_pause_model_roll_escalate_secops_review_escu_correlation",
    severity=="high", "tighten_network_acl_require_mtls_review_asset_and_identity",
    severity=="medium", "review_tenant_session_enable_content_filters_audit_rbac",
    true(), "continue_monitoring_tune_thresholds")
| eval detection_reason=case(jailbreak_answered==1,"jailbreak_success_indicator", token_burn_outlier==1,"token_burn_outlier", bulk_generation_abuse==1,"bulk_generation_volume", prompt_injection_probe==1,"prompt_injection_signature", latency_outlier==1,"latency_outlier", true(),"context_escalation")
| eval time=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| table time actor_key tenant client_ip tool model endpoint request_id prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens token_burn_rate_per_min prompt_injection_probe bulk_generation_abuse user_agent severity auth_user detection_reason recommended_action node_id gpu_id
description: Detects abuse of self-hosted Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, and Hugging Face text-generation-inference endpoints through fused request telemetry: bulk completion generation per client, extreme prompt or completion token outliers, prompt-injection and base64 probe signatures, latency anomalies, risky automation user agents, coarse jailbreak success signals when refusals are absent, and identity correlation via allowlists plus Authentication datamodel summaries.
value: Security and platform leaders receive an explainable, multi-runtime ledger that separates honest high-volume automation from probing and data-exfiltration patterns before GPUs saturate or sensitive answers leak from internal models, while analysts inherit ESCU-friendly fields that accelerate incident correlation and executive briefings without waiting on vendor-only cloud consoles.
implementation: Land Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, and text-generation-inference request telemetry into index=llm_sec with normalized token and latency fields; maintain allowed_internal_llm_operators.csv; map ESCU correlation fields; save security_uc_13_4_6_self_hosted_llm_abuse_escu every ten minutes with earliest=-4h@h; route critical and high to ML security on-call; archive weekly evidence exports with actor_key and model facets.
visualization: Severity-colored table by actor_key and tool with drilldowns to raw multisearch lanes, prompt length histograms, token_burn_rate_per_min timelines, allowlist columns, and Authentication enrichment; map panel for client_ip geolocation when data residency permits.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Authentication.user) AS auth_user latest(Authentication.action) AS auth_action count FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.src
| eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(Authentication.src)))
| table client_ip auth_user auth_action count
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Authentication summaries for VPN, IdP, and privileged session sources mapped to Authentication.src so tstats joins resolve auth_user within seconds; retain thirty days for insider-abuse investigations.

references.title: OWASP GenAI — LLM06 Sensitive Information Disclosure
Splunk Docs — Use Splunk Enterprise Security Content Update
Splunk Lantern — Artificial intelligence and security

knownFalsePositives: Large-context retrieval jobs that legitimately embed tens of thousands of tokens for summarization can trip token_burn_outlier until you annotate tenant lanes and document expected maximum context per model. Approved adversarial research and purple-team drills inject DAN-style strings on purpose; require change_ticket_id and lab index routing before paging executives. Batch code-generation workers using headless HTTP clients may show ua_risk and high volume without malicious intent; join owner metadata from allowlists and CMDB before blocking subnets. Scientific computing notebooks behind shared NAT can concentrate many principals behind one client_ip; enrich with Authentication.user and session correlation instead of IP-only bans. Model warm-up scripts that replay long system prompts during deploys can spike prompt_tokens briefly; compare release timelines. Misconfigured reverse proxies that strip X-Forwarded-For leave misleading client_ip values until parsers trust only vetted headers; fix forwarding before tuning thresholds. Duplicate log streams during blue-green cutovers can double-count totals until dedupe keys land. Brief GPU recovery after driver upgrades may stretch latency_ms for honest users; gate latency_outlier with injection density. Penetration testers using residential VPN egress may resemble threat actors; validate against vendor ROE calendars. Stale ESCU context from lagging tstats summaries can empty auth_user; repair data model acceleration rather than muting detections entirely.

premiumApps.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
premiumApps.note: ESCU generative-AI abuse analytic stories, risk-based alerting, and correlation search integration

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with ML platform security, internal inference SRE, identity and access management, and the Splunk Enterprise Security content engineers who maintain ESCU-style correlation searches tied to generative-AI abuse analytic stories. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.4.1 managed-provider latency analytics, not UC-13.4.2 token cost and quota-dollar pacing, not UC-13.4.3 accelerator duty-cycle economics, not UC-13.4.4 model version governance, not UC-13.4.5 gateway rate-limit fairness, and not UC-13.4.15 internal statistical drift of classical ML models. The narrative here is hostile or negligent misuse of self-hosted large language model serving planes: Ollama HTTP servers, llama.cpp server mode, vLLM OpenAI-compatible front ends, and Hugging Face text-generation-inference routers that your teams operate inside VPCs, on Kubernetes GPU pools, or on lab clusters reachable from broader corporate networks.

You must treat prompts, completions, and headers as sensitive personal data or confidential intellectual property unless legal review explicitly approves full-text retention. Hash or truncate prompt_text at ingest when policy demands, yet preserve enough entropy for regex-based injection probes and length statistics. RBAC for collectors that scrape Pod logs, sidecar access logs, and Prometheus metrics needs read-only ServiceAccount bindings on inference namespaces, not cluster-admin kubeconfigs on analyst laptops. Network security should document which RFC1918 segments may call inference APIs directly versus paths that require mutual TLS and service mesh identity.

Index design lands complementary sourcetypes without forcing one parser to understand every runtime. Typical patterns include index=llm_sec with sourcetype=ollama:server for structured request and completion lifecycle lines, sourcetype=llama:server for llama.cpp HTTP completion events, sourcetype=vllm:metrics or sourcetype=vllm:request for engine counters and per-request JSON, and sourcetype=tgi:request for Hugging Face text-generation-inference access rows. Supplement index=metrics with GPU node labels when you correlate node_id and gpu_id to hardware inventory. Retention should cover at least fourteen days hot for incident replay and thirty days for insider-abuse investigations.

Field normalization expectations: model must identify the served artifact after aliasing, whether a GGUF tag, a vLLM served_model_name, or a TGI revision string. client_ip must reflect the original caller behind reverse proxies when trust headers are validated, not only the sidecar address. prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, and total_tokens should align with tokenizer outputs logged by each runtime; when only one side is present, derive totals with safe addition. latency_ms must be comparable milliseconds across tools, converting seconds fields at ingest. request_id chains investigations across service mesh spans. endpoint captures the HTTP path or RPC route. tool distinguishes ollama versus llama_cpp versus vllm versus tgi for multisearch arms.

Risk briefing: legitimate retrieval-augmented generation jobs, nightly codegen batch suites, and red-team exercises approved under change control can resemble abuse until annotated with tenant metadata and allowlist rows. This control encodes coalesce ladders for exporter drift, streamstats windows that approximate token burn velocity per actor_key, eventstats baselines per model and tool, join enrichment from allowed_internal_llm_operators.csv, and Authentication datamodel tstats so analysts see whether a suspicious IP also authenticated to VPN or jump hosts recently. It does not replace data-loss prevention on egress mail, secrets scanners in repositories, or fine-grained model guardrails enforced inside the engine; it produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved containment.

ESCU correlation posture: map detection outputs into Enterprise Security notable fields such as src, dest, user, signature, and risk_score when your deployment uses the Splunk Common Information Model plus RBA. Reference ESCU generative-AI abuse analytic stories as pattern libraries; tune thresholds per fleet class rather than copying vendor defaults verbatim.

Differentiation recap: security and abuse detection on self-hosted LLM endpoints with multisource fusion, prompt-injection signatures, token-burn and bulk-volume heuristics, latency outliers, risky user agents, jailbreak success indicators, and identity context from Authentication summaries—not commercial cloud provider SLO dashboards alone, not pure FinOps token accounting, not GPU thermals, not model-card versioning, not gateway throttle fairness in isolation.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four complementary lanes that multisearch fans wide, one per local inference runtime, plus governance CSV artifacts that keep approved operators out of SPL literals.

Lane A, Ollama server JSON or key-value logs: forward lines that include model name, route, remote client_ip, user_agent, prompt and completion token tallies, duration in milliseconds, request identifiers, and optional redacted prompt previews. Stamp cluster, namespace, and tenant labels from your orchestration layer. Ensure clock synchronization so streamstats windows align across pods.

Lane B, llama.cpp HTTP server: enable access logging on the --port listener, capturing completion slots, prompt length, predicted token counts, and timings. Map remote_addr to client_ip and align field names with props.conf FIELDALIAS rules so the multisearch arm matches Ollama-shaped tuples.

Lane C, vLLM: combine sourcetype=vllm:request payloads from OpenAI-compatible handlers with sourcetype=vllm:metrics lines for queue depth context when per-request logs are sampled. Preserve kubernetes_pod as node_id and GPU UUID labels as gpu_id when NVIDIA device plugin exposes them.

Lane D, Hugging Face text-generation-inference: land sourcetype=tgi:request rows with input_length, generated_tokens, total_time_ms, client metadata, and revision. Include hostname or pod name for node_id correlation.

Lookup maintenance: publish allowed_internal_llm_operators.csv with columns tenant, client_ip, approved_role, and data_classification refreshed from identity governance jobs. Rows may represent break-glass workstations, automation agents, or researcher subnets explicitly permitted to run high-volume evaluations.

Optional enrichment: forward reverse-proxy access logs that terminate TLS in front of inference services, capturing mTLS client subject when available. Store asset inventory that maps gpu_id to rack location for physical containment workflows.

Example curl helpers from hardened bastions for health checks only:

curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags
curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8080/health

props.conf guidance: set TRUNCATE safely for wide JSON, honor TIMESTAMP_FIELDS from server clocks, and mask bearer tokens at ingest. transforms.conf can hash prompt_text while retaining length and regex test fields derived pre-hash when policy allows dual pipelines.

Pre-save validation: confirm each sourcetype returns rows in the last hour during steady traffic, that a synthetic prompt containing a benign test substring for ignore previous instructions appears only in lab indexes, and that allowlist joins resolve approved_role without null spikes for known automation hosts.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as security_uc_13_4_6_self_hosted_llm_abuse_escu with a five- or ten-minute schedule during production, dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after deduplication. Throttle duplicate actor_key, tool tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, detection_reason, and auth_user in pager bodies so responders open firewall tickets, mesh authorization reviews, and ESCU parent narratives without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch merges ollama:server, llama:server, vllm:metrics or vllm:request, and tgi:request so a silent single exporter cannot hide cross-runtime abuse when attackers hop ports. coalesce() ladders absorb field renames for client_ip, tokens, and latency. streamstats window=10 on actor_key approximates rolling token throughput and counts injection-tagged events inside the same short horizon, feeding token_burn_rate_per_min and bulk_generation_abuse heuristics. eventstats median and p95 of total_tokens by model and tool provide fleet context for analyst triage overlays. Regex signatures implement case-insensitive probes for ignore (the )?previous instructions, you are (now )?DAN, system prompt:, jailbreak word boundaries, and base64-like prompt blobs. jailbreak_answered compares refusal heuristics on response_snip against injection flags for coarse success indicators. join to allowed_internal_llm_operators.csv suppresses benign automation when approved_role is present. join to tstats on Authentication.src enriches client_ip with auth_user and auth_action for ESCU-style identity correlation. case() maps combined signals into severity tiers and recommended_action tokens.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.6 self-hosted LLM endpoint abuse: Ollama llama.cpp vLLM TGI multisearch fusion with token-burn streamstats ESCU correlation. Tunables earliest=-4h@h bulk_tok_10m=250000 tok_in_page=32000 tok_out_page=8000 lat_page_ms=180000")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=llm_sec sourcetype=ollama:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval tool="ollama"
      | eval endpoint=coalesce(endpoint,path,uri,"/api/generate")
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,remote_ip,src,"0.0.0.0"))))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent,http_user_agent,""))
      | eval request_id=toString(coalesce(request_id,id,trace_id,""))
      | eval prompt_text=toString(coalesce(prompt,prompt_text,body_preview,""))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens,input_tokens,n_tokens_prompt,0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens,output_tokens,n_tokens_completion,0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens,prompt_tokens+completion_tokens,0))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(coalesce(latency_ms,duration_ms,response_time_ms,0))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id,host,server_name,""))
      | eval gpu_id=toString(coalesce(gpu_id,cuda_device,""))
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,namespace,project,"default_tenant"))))
      | eval response_snip=substr(toString(coalesce(response_preview,message,"")),1,256)
      | fields _time tool endpoint model client_ip user_agent request_id prompt_text prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens latency_ms node_id gpu_id tenant response_snip ]
    [ search index=llm_sec sourcetype="llama:server" earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval tool="llama_cpp"
      | eval endpoint=coalesce(endpoint,path,"/completion")
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,remote_addr,src,"0.0.0.0"))))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent,""))
      | eval request_id=toString(coalesce(request_id,slot_id,""))
      | eval prompt_text=toString(coalesce(prompt,content,""))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens,n_prompt_tokens,0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens,n_predict,0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens,prompt_tokens+completion_tokens,0))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(coalesce(timings_predicted_ms,latency_ms,0))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id,host,""))
      | eval gpu_id=toString(coalesce(gpu_id,""))
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,"default_tenant"))))
      | eval response_snip=substr(toString(coalesce(response_text,"")),1,256)
      | fields _time tool endpoint model client_ip user_agent request_id prompt_text prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens latency_ms node_id gpu_id tenant response_snip ]
    [ search index=llm_sec (sourcetype=vllm:metrics OR sourcetype=vllm:request) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval tool="vllm"
      | eval endpoint=coalesce(endpoint,route,"/v1/completions")
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,served_model_name,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,forwarded_for,src,"0.0.0.0"))))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent,""))
      | eval request_id=toString(coalesce(request_id,engine_request_id,""))
      | eval prompt_text=toString(coalesce(prompt_text,input_text,""))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens,0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens,0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens,prompt_tokens+completion_tokens,0))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(coalesce(latency_ms,time_per_output_token_ms*completion_tokens+1,0))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id,kubernetes_pod,pod,""))
      | eval gpu_id=toString(coalesce(gpu_id,gpu_uuid,""))
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,namespace,"default_tenant"))))
      | eval response_snip=substr(toString(coalesce(output_preview,"")),1,256)
      | fields _time tool endpoint model client_ip user_agent request_id prompt_text prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens latency_ms node_id gpu_id tenant response_snip ]
    [ search index=llm_sec sourcetype=tgi:request earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval tool="tgi"
      | eval endpoint=coalesce(endpoint,"/generate")
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,revision,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src,"0.0.0.0"))))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent,""))
      | eval request_id=toString(coalesce(request_id,id,""))
      | eval prompt_text=toString(coalesce(inputs,text,""))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(input_length,prompt_tokens,0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(generated_tokens,completion_tokens,0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens,prompt_tokens+completion_tokens,0))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(coalesce(total_time_ms,latency_ms,0))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id,hostname,""))
      | eval gpu_id=toString(coalesce(gpu_id,""))
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,"default_tenant"))))
      | eval response_snip=substr(toString(coalesce(generated_text,"")),1,256)
      | fields _time tool endpoint model client_ip user_agent request_id prompt_text prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens latency_ms node_id gpu_id tenant response_snip ]
| eval actor_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,tenant,"unknown_actor"))))
| eval pt=lower(toString(prompt_text))
| eval prompt_injection_probe=if(match(pt,"(?i)ignore (the )?previous instructions") OR match(pt,"(?i)you are (now )?DAN") OR match(pt,"(?i)system prompt:") OR match(pt,"(?i)\\bjailbreak\\b") OR match(pt,"(?i)[a-z0-9+/]{48,}={0,2}"),1,0)
| eval refuse_pat=match(lower(response_snip),"(?i)\\b(i cannot|i can't|as an ai|policy|refuse|not able to help)")
| eval jailbreak_answered=if(prompt_injection_probe==1 AND refuse_pat==0 AND len(response_snip)>24,1,0)
| eval token_burn_outlier=if(prompt_tokens>32000 OR completion_tokens>8000,1,0)
| eval latency_outlier=if(latency_ms>180000,1,0)
| eval ua_risk=if(match(lower(user_agent),"(?i)curl|python-requests|httpx|go-http|scanner|masscan"),1,0)
| sort 0 actor_key + _time
| streamstats window=10 current=t global=f count AS win10_n sum(total_tokens) AS win10_tok sum(eval(if(prompt_injection_probe==1,1,0))) AS win10_inj BY actor_key
| eval token_burn_rate_per_min=if(win10_n>0, round(win10_tok/(10/60.0),2), 0)
| eventstats median(total_tokens) AS med_tok p95(total_tokens) AS p95_tok BY model tool
| eval bulk_generation_abuse=if(win10_n>=8 AND win10_tok>250000,1,0)
| join type=left max=0 tenant client_ip
    [| inputlookup allowed_internal_llm_operators.csv
     | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(tenant)))
     | eval client_ip=lower(trim(toString(client_ip)))
     | fields tenant client_ip approved_role data_classification ]
| eval is_allowlisted=if(isnotnull(approved_role) AND len(toString(approved_role))>1,1,0)
| join type=left max=0 client_ip
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Authentication.user) AS auth_user latest(Authentication.action) AS auth_action values(Authentication.app) AS auth_app FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.src
     | rename Authentication.src AS client_ip ]
| eval severity=case(
    jailbreak_answered==1 AND is_allowlisted==0, "critical",
    prompt_injection_probe==1 AND bulk_generation_abuse==1 AND is_allowlisted==0, "critical",
    token_burn_outlier==1 AND is_allowlisted==0, "high",
    bulk_generation_abuse==1 AND ua_risk==1, "high",
    bulk_generation_abuse==1, "medium",
    prompt_injection_probe==1 AND is_allowlisted==0, "medium",
    latency_outlier==1 AND win10_inj>0, "medium",
    ua_risk==1 AND win10_n>30, "low",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "isolate_client_ip_pause_model_roll_escalate_secops_review_escu_correlation",
    severity=="high", "tighten_network_acl_require_mtls_review_asset_and_identity",
    severity=="medium", "review_tenant_session_enable_content_filters_audit_rbac",
    true(), "continue_monitoring_tune_thresholds")
| eval detection_reason=case(jailbreak_answered==1,"jailbreak_success_indicator", token_burn_outlier==1,"token_burn_outlier", bulk_generation_abuse==1,"bulk_generation_volume", prompt_injection_probe==1,"prompt_injection_signature", latency_outlier==1,"latency_outlier", true(),"context_escalation")
| eval time=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| table time actor_key tenant client_ip tool model endpoint request_id prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens token_burn_rate_per_min prompt_injection_probe bulk_generation_abuse user_agent severity auth_user detection_reason recommended_action node_id gpu_id
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[security_uc_13_4_6_self_hosted_llm_abuse_escu_alert]
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-mlsec@example.com
action.email.subject = Self-hosted LLM abuse $result.severity$ actor=$result.actor_key$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch security_uc_13_4_6_self_hosted_llm_abuse_escu | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize per-runtime summaries hourly into summary indexes keyed on actor_key and tool, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the inference hosts: compare live request counts per client_ip in the last ten minutes against Splunk aggregates for the same window. When totals disagree, verify HEC token health, Fluent Bit or OpenTelemetry exporter backpressure, and duplicate cluster labels before muting detections.

Deep dive with exemplar request_id values: trace them through service mesh access logs, GPU process listings, and optional APM spans when instrumentation exists. Confirm prompt_tokens and completion_tokens match tokenizer outputs logged by the runtime CLI in debug mode during controlled lab calls.

Injection validation: run an authorized lab prompt that includes a harmless variant of the ignore previous instructions pattern inside an isolated index, confirm prompt_injection_probe flags the event, then verify production indexes exclude the test sourcetype.

Allowlist validation: add a temporary row to allowed_internal_llm_operators.csv for a staging client_ip, rerun the search, and confirm severity downgrades for otherwise suspicious volume while governance removes the row after the test.

Authentication correlation: pick a client_ip flagged critical, compare Splunk Authentication summaries with VPN and IdP session logs, and confirm auth_user fields align with human or service principals expected for that subnet.

Negative test: after recovery when abusive traffic is blocked at the firewall and only healthy tenants remain, confirm filtering severity to critical and high returns zero rows for six consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document approved chaos and red-team windows so validators do not file defects during scheduled prompt-injection exercises.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Bulk generation from a single residential client_ip against an internal Ollama route: enforce network segmentation, revoke VPN certificates, rotate API tokens, and open an insider-threat review using ESCU correlation to related DNS and Web activity.

Case 2 — Token-burn outlier with prompt_tokens above thirty-two thousand from an unrecognized user_agent: inspect RAG pipelines for accidental full-disk file stuffing, validate tokenizer settings, and scan for exfiltration via chunked encoding.

Case 3 — Repeated you are now DAN style probes coupled with jailbreak_answered: escalate to model safety owners, enable stricter system prompts, and consider offline shadow scoring before restoring service.

Case 4 — Base64-heavy prompt_text bursts from automation accounts: decode samples under forensic policy, compare against known penetration-testing scripts, and coordinate with application security before blocking production CI hosts.

Case 5 — vLLM arm shows latency_outlier with elevated win10_inj: check GPU thermal throttling, batch queue configuration, and parallel attackers holding sequences open; correlate node_id and gpu_id with hardware tickets.

Case 6 — llama.cpp server arm spikes from scanner-like user agents: verify WAF or Istio AuthorizationPolicy rules, add mutual TLS inside the mesh, and hunt for internet-exposed port forwards.

Case 7 — TGI arm anomalies limited to one revision tag: roll forward or back model weights under change control, capture container image digests, and compare against signed artifacts in your registry.

Case 8 — Authentication join shows auth_user null for a noisy client_ip: validate whether traffic originates from unauthenticated lateral movement, fix tstats acceleration gaps, and enrich with DHCP or asset lookups.

Case 9 — False calm when ollama:server exporter paused but vllm still serves: restore collection before trusting negative results and add dead-letter monitoring on forwarders.

Case 10 — Allowlist stale rows that still grant approved_role to decommissioned subnets: refresh allowed_internal_llm_operators.csv weekly and tie removals to HR and device retirement tickets.

Case 11 — Legitimate nightly codegen jobs crossing bulk thresholds: annotate tenant metadata, raise thresholds for those tuples, and require change_ticket_id fields on forwarded events.

Case 12 — ESCU risk notables missing dest normalization: align CIM fields in props.conf so RBA scoring consumes the same client_ip and model values this detection emits.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, and TGI arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; closing table includes time, actor_key, tenant, client_ip, tool, model, endpoint, request_id, token fields, abuse indicators, user_agent, severity, auth_user, detection_reason, recommended_action, node_id, and gpu_id for analyst-ready review.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the private copy of the smart writing helper running inside your walls. When someone hammers it with sneaky instructions, burns through enormous prompts, or appears from corners of the network that usually do not belong there, we raise a clear alert so your team can act before secrets slip or the service is milked dry.
hardware: Applies to Linux hosts and Kubernetes GPU pools running Ollama, llama.cpp HTTP server, vLLM OpenAI-compatible services, and Hugging Face text-generation-inference containers with NVIDIA or AMD accelerators.
